zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Aske於大約1378年出生在英國越可爾奧頓時，她的父親John de Aske47歲，母親Joan Shelvestrode37歲。她和Richard Hansard I至少有3個兒子。她去世於1435年2月8日之後在英國達勒姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Aske于大约1378年出生在英国越可爾奧頓时，她的父亲John de Aske47岁，母亲Joan Shelvestrode37岁。她和Richard Hansard I至少有3个儿子。她去世于1435年2月8日之后在英国達勒姆。

nameMeanings.name: Aske
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Staffordshire): topographic name from northern Middle English ask ‘ash tree’ (Old Norse askr), or a habitational name from Aske (North Yorkshire). Compare Ash .

variants.name: Ash
meanings: English: from Middle English asche ‘ash tree’ (Old English æsc), hence a topographic name for someone living by an ash tree or a habitational name from any of the many places in southern and central England named with this word (Derbyshire, Dorset, Hampshire, Herefordshire, Kent, Surrey, Shropshire, Somerset, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of German   Asch   and   Esch  , the latter ultimately also of Swiss German origin (see   Oesch  ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dufresne  , with the same meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Axe
meanings: English (West Yorkshire, Lincolnshire): variant of Aske with metathesis of /sk/ to /ks/, spelled with an x.

English (Dorset, Somerset): habitational name from Axe Farm in Chardstock (Devon, formerly in Dorset), itself named for the river Axe, which forms part of the south-eastern boundary of Chardstock parish.
Americanized form of German   Axt   or perhaps of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Aske was born about 1378, in Aughton, Yorkshire, England, her father, John de Aske, was 47 and her mother, Joan Shelvestrode, was 37. She had at least 3 sons with Richard Hansard I. She died after 8 February 1435, in Durham, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Aske est née environ 1378, à Aughton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John de Aske, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Joan Shelvestrode, avait 37 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils avec Richard Hansard I. Elle est décédée après 8 février 1435, à Durham, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Aske um 1378 in Aughton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Aske 47 und ihre Mutter Joan Shelvestrode 37. Sie hatte mit Richard Hansard I mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb nach 8. Februar 1435 in Durham, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Aske è nata il circa 1378 nel Aughton, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John de Aske, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Joan Shelvestrode, aveva 37 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli con Richard Hansard I. Morì dopo il 8 febbraio 1435, nel Durham, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Askeが約1378年にイギリスヨークシュアイアアウトンで生まれた時，父John de Askeは47歳，母Joan Shelvestrodeは37歳であった。彼女はRichard Hansard Iとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1435年2月8日以後にイギリスダルハムで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Aske(이)가 대략 1378년 영국 요르크실르 오그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Aske(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Shelvestrode(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 Richard Hansard I(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1435년 2월 8일 이후 영국 뒤람에서 사망했다.

mn.about 1378–after 8 February 1435: ойролцоогоор 1378 он–1435 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 8–наас хойш

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Aske nasceu aproximadamente 1378, em Aughton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Aske, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Joan Shelvestrode, tinha 37 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Richard Hansard I. Ela faleceu após 8 de fevereiro de 1435, em Durham, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Aske родилась около 1378 г. в местности аугтон, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Aske было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Shelvestrode, 37. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына от Richard Hansard I. Она умерла после 8 февраль 1435 г. в местности дурам, Англия.

sm.about 1378–after 8 February 1435: e tusa o le 1378–ina ua ma'ea le 8 Fepuari 1435

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Aske nació aproximadamente 1378, en Aughton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Aske, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Joan Shelvestrode, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Richard Hansard I. Murió después de 8 de febrero de 1435, en Durham, Inglaterra.
